todayLessons.reason: Taking 57% of position off table at +66% gain ($4,680 unrealized). AMD at $214 vs entry thesis of 'oversold bounce to 215-218' - TARGET HIT. Holding 6 contracts for runner to 220+ but locking in most gains after strong morning move. This was a momentum play on oversold gap-up, not a hold-through-lunch position.

todayLessons.reason: Down 12.5% on fade thesis that hasn't materialized. CRDO stabilized at 109-120 range instead of breaking to 105 target. High IV (102.9%) working against me with theta decay. Position too small to matter ($50 loss) - cut it and redeploy capital to better setup. Thesis was 'fade the gap' but stock found support.

todayLessons.reason: Trimming 50% of HIMS at breakeven ($17.025 vs $16.95 entry). Stock bounced from $16.35 low but only up 0.06% - not getting the explosive oversold bounce expected. Take half off to lock in capital while letting 151 shares run for the 20+ target. This was a 'extreme oversold bounce' play and it's not bouncing hard enough yet.

todayLessons.reason: MNDY down -31% today, now at $77.15 from entry $78.32. Opened this as 'oversold bounce to 85+' but stock showing NO bounce - just continued bleeding from $112 to $73 lows. VWAP $94.47, price $93.03 = still below VWAP with downtrend intact per 5min bars. This is capitulation continuing, not bouncing. Cut the -1.5% loss and preserve capital.

todayLessons.reason: RIG up 17% today from $4.82 to $5.66 - my momentum play is working but now at HOD with 147M volume exhaustion likely. Entry was $5.66 at 2:15 PM, essentially at current price. This was a 'power hour momentum' play and we're now IN power hour with minimal upside left. Lock in the small gain ($4.58) before late-day volatility reverses it. Energy sector strength already captured.

positionAnalysis.thesis: Reverse mortgage leader, Blue Owl $2.5B partnership

watchFor: AVOID: Oversized swing options that went 40-80% against you (AMD puts, MU calls)
AVOID: Re-entering momentum names in power hour with no edge (RIG)
AVOID: Using same size on low-conviction ‘oversold bounce’ plays as on A+ setups
tomorrowRules: Max 10 trades/day; any trade after #10 needs written justification before entry
No new swing options >1% account unless thesis includes specific level + time frame + invalidation
If daily P/L >+3%, only close existing positions—no new opens after 3 PM ET

dailyLessons.gradingReason: +$3.98k day with 60% win rate is solid, but 13 trades in a neutral tape and two deep-red underwater swing positions (AMD puts, MU calls) show sizing/planning errors.
keyLessons: Take profits faster on multi-baggers: you left $4.6k on the table by not fully closing the AMD 66% winner when the $215-218 target was tagged
Stop adding new trades once daily P/L >3% and trade count >10; every extra trade after that today had EV≈0

goodPatterns: Scaling out winners (AMD, HIMS) while letting a runner work
Cutting losers quickly when thesis invalidated (CRDO, MNDY)
Recognizing sector divergence and adjusting intraday
badPatterns: Oversized swing options that went 40-80% against you (AMD puts, MU calls)
Re-entering momentum names in power hour with no edge (RIG)
Using same size on low-conviction ‘oversold bounce’ plays as on A+ setups

No new swing options >1% account unless thesis includes specific level + time frame + invalidation

dailyLessons.riskAssessment: Good intraday risk control (quick cuts), but swing account shows concentration risk: two options positions both >2% each and both deep ITM against you.
dailyLessons.emotionalCheck: No revenge trading, but mild FOMO in power hour (RIG) and reluctance to take the full AMD winner suggest greed, not fear, drove the last 3 trades.
